title: The Device I Can't Stop Raving About Is On Sale for Prime Day
gist: WIRED editor recommendation of Skylight's digital wall calendar (Calendar 2 at $290, Calendar Max at $530) during Prime Day. Key commercial detail: Skylight requires a $79/year subscription (Skylight Plus) for core features including meal planning, photo mode, and smart calendar import—making this a hardware-plus-subscription play.

insights.claim: Skylight's digital wall calendar requires a $79/year subscription (Skylight Plus) to access core features including meal planning, photo screensaver mode, and Magic Import email-to-calendar conversion—without the subscription, the device loses significant functionality.
insights.speaker: Nena Farrell
insights.so_what: Skylight's subscription-gated feature model on a hardware device priced $290-$630 mirrors the broader smart home industry trend of recurring revenue through feature paywalls—relevant for evaluating hardware+SaaS valuations in consumer tech.
insights.quote: Skylight Plus ($79 a year) is required to get access to all of the features on Skylight's calendars, including meal planning, photo mode, and Magic Import

insights.claim: The Skylight Calendar Max at 27 inches and $530 (16% off from $630) represents the high end of the family digital display market, distinct from standard digital photo frames—competing in a premium household organization device category.
insights.speaker: Nena Farrell
insights.so_what: A $530 household organizational display device achieving editorial recommendation signals this is a category with real consumer willingness-to-pay beyond basic smart displays.
